svg.text: Detailed technical illustrations — stainless steel instruments shown to relative scale
Large curved belly — general skin incisions
Small curved belly — fine incisions, pediatric, ENT
Triangular point — stab incisions, port sites, arteriotomy
Heavy blades, blunt tip — fascia, heavy suture
Long slender blades, slightly curved — delicate tissue dissection
Never cut suture with Metz — ruins the blade
Monopolar ESU — cut and coagulate modes
Pass tip-first in neutral zone; never palm-to-palm
Simultaneous cut + vessel seal up to 7 mm — laparoscopic dissection
Seals vessels WITHOUT clips; less smoke than monopolar
Medium; half-serrated jaw — clamping vessels and pedicles
Half-serrated = serrations on distal half only
Heavy; fully serrated jaw with 1×2 teeth at tip — dense tissue
1×2 tooth at the tip = toothed Kocher
Used for fascia, pedicles, heavy tissue
Multiple interlocking teeth at tip — grips tissue to be excised
Traumatic — tissue grasped is usually excised
NOT for bowel — use Babcock instead
Smooth fenestrated oval jaw — atraumatic bowel and tube grasping
Fenestration visible when jaw is open
Atraumatic serrated jaw — vessels and bowel
Never use on skin — atraumatic only
Double-ended S-shape — skin and subcutaneous tissue
S-shape = can retract in either direction
Hooked blade, flat handle — abdominal wall
Spring self-retaining with sharp prongs — small incisions
Orthopedic · small wounds · no assistant needed
Rigid curved tube, bulbous tip — oropharynx, general
Cover vent = full suction · Uncover = off
Thin angled tip — neuro, ENT, ear, small spaces
Available in multiple gauges (4 Fr – 12 Fr)
Neurosurgery · ENT · ear · deep wound tracking
Fenestrated sleeve over inner cannula — large volume fluid removal
Sleeve slides off inner cannula for cleaning
Long shaft with footplate + punch jaw — bite bone
2 mm, 3 mm, 4 mm sizes; up-cut and down-cut
Sharp bone retractor — THA, TKA, acetabulum
Long shaft with cup jaws — disc space, nuclear material
All instruments are stainless steel unless otherwise noted. Sizes vary by manufacturer. Illustrations show representative geometry — not exact product dimensions.
